changes:
Rewrote src/dockym/engine/client.py with the following improvements:

1. THREAD SAFETY: Added threading.Lock around all global client state mutations. The original code had race conditions since QThreadPool workers (ScanTask, ComposeTask) call get_client() from multiple threads without synchronization.

2. CONNECTION LEAK PREVENTION: Added _close_client_safely() helper that calls client.close() to release the underlying requests.Session connection pool. This is called before every client replacement — the original code set _client=None without closing, leaking HTTP connections.

3. TTL-BASED HEALTH CHECKS: Changed from ping-on-every-call to a 30s TTL (HEALTH_CHECK_TTL). The fast path returns the cached client immediately without network overhead. Original pinged on every single get_client() call.

4. EXPONENTIAL BACKOFF: Replaced the fixed 5s retry interval with exponential backoff (5s base, 60s cap). Uses time.monotonic() instead of time.time() to avoid clock-jump issues.

5. EXPLICIT CLEANUP API: Added close_client() for application shutdown — calls client.close() and resets state. Original had no way to cleanly shut down.

6. CONNECTION POOL CONFIGURATION: Added timeout=5s and max_pool_size=10 to both from_env() and DockerClient() constructors to prevent hanging on unresponsive daemons.

7. BETTER ERROR HANDLING: Added specific exception types (APIError, NotFound) in addition to DockerException. Separated _create_client() from get_client() for cleaner flow.

8. STRUCTURED LOGGING: Added logging.getLogger(__name__) with appropriate log levels (info for connections, warning for health check failures, debug for skipped containers).

9. FIXED is_docker_available(): The original called ping() twice (once inside get_client, once after). Now delegates entirely to get_client() which handles its own health check.

All existing public functions preserved with identical signatures. New export: close_client().
review_request: Check for connection leak patterns: verify that _close_client_safely is called in every code path where _client is replaced or set to None. Also review thread-safety of the _create_client() function which modifies _client global under _lock.
